acceptance_targets: [chapter_07_prose_001] Pass if each cited passage reads as grammatically complete narration with explicit subjects/articles while preserving Mira's close POV; fail if any of those beats still relies on standalone noun-phrase fragments such as 'Heat, sweat, involuntary tremor.' to create force.

[chapter_07_craft_001] Pass if, before the first kiss or at the latest before Mira climbs onto the table, the revised text gives Mira one clear threshold beat naming the room's leftover clinical or trauma residue and her decision to cross that line with Tavi; fail if the scene still moves from cleanup into sex without that conscious crossing appearing on the page.

Y.' correction beat across the cited passages, and each remaining beat distinguishes itself by a different syntactic landing; fail if multiple cited moments still resolve through the same negative-correction formula.

[FBR-004] Pass if both cited spans no longer use the stillness formula and the replacement reactions could not be swapped into chapters 12, 15, or 16 unchanged; fail if either beat still relies on generic stillness/flatness wording.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each cited passage reads as grammatically complete narration with explicit subjects/articles while preserving Mira's close POV; fail if any of those beats still relies on standalone noun-phrase fragments such as 'Heat, sweat, involuntary tremor.' to create force.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly uses article-stripped noun fragments and clipped shorthand such as 'Unauthorized commercial sex. Tissue tear.' and 'Work. Not pardon. Not dismissal.' This violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping and turns compression into a chapter-wide prose habit instead of controlled variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the clipped narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:289, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:343, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:487 as fully grammatical sentences still anchored to Mira's pressure and rhythm. Keep the compression, but make the syntax carry it by letting Mira state the debt, sensation, or work/not-pardon distinction in complete sentences rather than noun-stack fragments.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, before the first kiss or at the latest before Mira climbs onto the table, the revised text gives Mira one clear threshold beat naming the room's leftover clinical or trauma residue and her decision to cross that line with Tavi; fail if the scene still moves from cleanup into sex without that conscious crossing appearing on the page.
findings.problem: The chapter asks the reader to follow Mira and Tavi from repairing Luz's sexual trauma to having sex in the same back room, but Mira never consciously marks that threshold. Because the room still carries solvent, sweet-rot, blood cleanup, and clinic hardware, the pivot can read as under-acknowledged scene convenience instead of a deliberately chosen transgressive crossing.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the transition around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:197, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:205, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:217, and before the table setup at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:275, add one explicit Mira beat that registers the residual clinic reality and chooses intimacy anyway. The scene should show that the charge comes from months of shared labor and bodily familiarity, not from Luz's injury becoming accidental foreplay.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter uses no more than one 'Not X / Because Y' or 'Not X. Y.' correction beat across the cited passages, and each remaining beat distinguishes itself by a different syntactic landing; fail if multiple cited moments still resolve through the same negative-correction formula.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same negative-correction emphasis pattern: 'Not because... Because...', 'Not performatively slow. Real slow.', 'Not metaphor.', 'Not from sex.' Repeated across both scenes, the move stops feeling incisive and starts announcing emotion in the same cadence every time.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the emphatic turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:85, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:333, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:353, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:367, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:481 so that most of them land through different sentence shapes, image, or action. Keep at most one explicit negative-correction pair in the sex/confession run; let the rest of the emphasis arrive via uninterrupted syntax or concrete sensory consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited spans no longer use the stillness formula and the replacement reactions could not be swapped into chapters 12, 15, or 16 unchanged; fail if either beat still relies on generic stillness/flatness wording.
findings.problem: The book's recurring stillness pattern appears across 6 chapters and 8 instances; this chapter contributes two of the weaker uses at lines 3357 and 3481. Because Lin's sanctioned stillness elsewhere already carries the motif, using the same wording here for erotic shock and betrayal flattens reaction into a reusable book-default.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the stillness beats at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3355-3359 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3475-3483. Give Tavi and Mira chapter-specific physical reactions instead: hands hesitating, breath failing, cabinet/tile contact, clothing readjustment, joke collapse, or jaw/eye behavior tied to this room and this aftermath. Do not use 'went still' or 'went flat' phrasing in these beats.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into biotech grime, erotic awkwardness, debt language, bureaucratic absurdity, and the tenderness of people maintaining one another under bad systems. When choosing between an elegant abstraction and an ugly specific detail, choose the specific detail unless omission itself heightens pressure.
aesthetic_risk_policy.edge_case_resolution: Use restraint only when aftermath, gesture, residue, or changed behavior keeps the full pressure visible. Do not convert morally compromising acts into self-exonerating reflection, and do not flatten exploitative sex, poverty, or violence into generic dystopian mood.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches injury, sex, class shame, or institutional cruelty and the language retreats into tasteful fog, rewrite until the bodily fact, immediate sensation, and social consequence are fully legible on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the norm in spoken English here; uncontracted forms are reserved for legal testimony, managerial performance, deliberate emphasis, or characters whose formality is itself a tactic.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate inspection-room procedural chapters with crowded comic clinic scenes, quiet domestic intervals, erotic pressure, warehouse horror, and civic hearing sequences; consecutive chapters should differ in scene density, dialogue load, and emotional temperature so the book keeps changing how it moves.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, urban, bodily, and unsentimental; technical vocabulary is welcome when embedded in action and cost, not as decorative futurism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third-person close anchored to Mira Alves; the narration can infer social systems through what she notices, but it should not become omniscient exposition.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate crisp claim-note precision with longer, salt-wet, bodily sentences when feeling, memory, or city pressure breaches Mira's control.
